zh.lifeSummary: 當Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten於1180年出生時，她的父親Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten51歲，母親Christina Van Schoten31歲。她和Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg至少有6個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1234年享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten于1180年出生时，她的父亲Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten51岁，母亲Christina Van Schoten31岁。她和Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg至少有6个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1234年享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Breda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Dino, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Geno, Massimo, Quintino, Sante, Ugo.

Italian: topographic name from medieval Latin braida ‘grassy meadow, flat grassland adjoining a settlement’, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, such as the districts (now suburbs) of Genoa, Milan, Mantua, and Brescia called Breda or Brera.
Dutch: habitational name fromthe city of this name in North Brabant.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 岑, referring to ‘small high hills’ or ‘precipice’ in Chinese: (i) from the placename Cen (岑), the name of a fief (located in Hancheng in present-day Shaanxi province), which was granted to Qu, nephew of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ), by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (ii) for some families, this surname can be traced back to certain minority ethnic groups in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Schoten
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Dirk, Bastian, Berend, Gradus, Harmen, Hendrick, Jacobus, Willem, Wim.
Dutch and German: patronymic from the status name Scholte ‘village headman’, a variant of Schulte .

variants.name: Schulte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Dieter, Heinz, Rainer, Alois, Dietmar, Franz, Fritz, Gerhard, Hans.

North German and Dutch: status name from Middle Low German schulthete, Middle Dutch schulte ‘village headman’. In the northwestern Germany the name denotes the holder of a large farm. His status came with the obligation to make certain legal decisions. Compare Schulz , Schultheis .

en.lifeSummary: When Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten was born in 1180, her father, Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten, was 51 and her mother, Christina Van Schoten, was 31. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg. She died in 1234, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten est née en 1180, son père, Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Christina Van Schoten, avait 31 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg. Elle est décédée en 1234, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten 1180 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten 51 und ihre Mutter Christina Van Schoten 31. Sie hatte mit Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1234 im Alter von 54 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten è nata il nel 1180, sua padre, Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Christina Van Schoten, aveva 31 anni. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia con Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg. Morì nel 1234, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Kunigunde van Breda en Schotenが1180年に生まれた時，父Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schotenは51歳，母Christina Van Schotenは31歳であった。彼女はGraf Burchard I von Oldenburgとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1234年に54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten(이)가 1180년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Christina Van Schoten(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1234년 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten nasceu em 1180, seu pai, Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Christina Van Schoten, tinha 31 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha com Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg. Ela faleceu em 1234, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten родилась в 1180 г., её отцу по имени Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Christina Van Schoten, 31. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg. Она умерла в 1234 г. в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Kunigunde van Breda en Schoten nació en 1180, su papá, Heer Hendrik II van Breda en Schoten, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Christina Van Schoten, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija con Graf Burchard I von Oldenburg. Murió en 1234, a la edad de 54 años.
